faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth problem in my Island Park home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include small holes in wool or silk clothing, webbing in closet corners, flying moths near wardrobes, and silky cocoons on fabric surfaces. Island Park's coastal humidity creates ideal breeding conditions, so regular closet inspections are recommended, especially in older homes near the waterways.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth treatment cost in Island Park?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control in Island Park ranges from $550 for minor infestations in a single room to $5,000 for severe whole-home treatments. Pricing depends on the size of your home, infestation severity, number of affected areas, and whether treatment includes closets, storage areas, or entire living spaces.

faqs.question: How long does clothes moth extermination take in Island Park homes?
faqs.answer: Initial treatment typically takes 2-4 hours depending on home size and infestation extent. Most Island Park homes require a follow-up visit within 2-3 weeks to ensure complete elimination of eggs and larvae. Our technicians will provide a customized treatment schedule based on your specific situation.

faqs.question: Can clothes moths damage more than just clothing?
faqs.answer: Yes, clothes moths feed on any natural fibers including carpets, upholstery, curtains, blankets, and even stuffed furniture. In Island Park homes, we often find infestations in stored seasonal clothing, vintage textiles, and wool area rugs. They're particularly attracted to items with food stains, perspiration, or body oils.
